statements.name: PROC STYLEGAN
statements.description: The STYLEGAN Procedure trains a Style GAN model on images in SAS Viya. PROC STYLEGAN implements the Style GAN version 2 model by using the PyTorch library.
statements.help: PROC STYLEGAN IMAGESIZE=number<options>; OPTIMIZATION <options>; OUTPUT OUT=libref.data-table; SAVESTATE RSTORE=libref.data-table; TRAIN <options>;

arguments.name: IMAGESIZE=
arguments.description: specifies the image size (width of square images in pixels) in the training data

arguments.name: DATA=
arguments.description: names the input data table for PROC STYLEGAN to use

arguments.name: DETERMINISTIC
arguments.description: DETERMINISTIC} tells the NVIDIA CUDA Deep Neural Network library (cuDNN) that you want only the deterministic implementations

arguments.name: NUMSAMPLES=
arguments.description: specifies the number of sample images to generate

arguments.name: SCORESEED=
arguments.description: specifies the seed to use for the random number generator for scoring

arguments.name: SEED=
arguments.description: specifies the seed to use for the random number generator for training

arguments.name: USEGPU
arguments.description: specifies that a GPU device is to be used

arguments.name: DEVICE=
arguments.description: specifies the ID number of the GPU device that is to be used

statements.name: OPTIMIZATION
statements.description: specifies the optimizer parameters to use in training the style GAN model

arguments.name: ADAM
arguments.description: specifies the optimization method to use in training the GAN model

arguments.name: BETA1=
arguments.description: specifies the exponential decay rate for the first-moment estimates

arguments.name: BETA2=
arguments.description: specifies the exponential decay rate for the second-moment estimates

arguments.name: LEARNINGRATE=
arguments.description: specifies the learning rate for the optimizer

arguments.name: MINIBATCHSIZE=
arguments.description: specifies the number of images in one minibatch

arguments.name: NUMITERS=
arguments.description: specifies the number of minibatches to be used for training the GAN model

statements.name: OUTPUT
statements.description: creates an output data table that contains the results of PROC STYLEGAN

arguments.name: OUT=
arguments.description: specifies the output table in which to store the generated images from the trained model

statements.name: SAVESTATE
statements.description: creates an analytic store for the model and saves it as a binary object in a data table

arguments.name: RSTORE=
arguments.description: specifies a data table in which to save the analytic store for the model

statements.name: TRAIN
statements.description: specifies the training parameters to use for training the style GAN model

arguments.name: CHECKPOINTFREQ=
arguments.description: specifies the frequency at which to save a checkpoint during the training

arguments.name: CHECKPOINTIN=
arguments.description: specifies the checkpoint table to be loaded to restore your model

arguments.name: CHECKPOINTOUT=
arguments.description: specifies the table in which to save the current model as a checkpoint

arguments.name: NTRUNCATIONLATENTS=
arguments.description: specifies the number of latents to use in calculating the truncation mean

arguments.name: PRINTFREQ=
arguments.description: specifies the frequency at which to print losses to the log and the iteration history table

arguments.name: REGDFREQ=
arguments.description: specifies the frequency at which to regularize the discriminator

arguments.name: REGGFREQ=
arguments.description: specifies the frequency at which to regularize the generator

arguments.name: REGPATHWEIGHT=
arguments.description: specifies the weight for regularizing the path length loss

arguments.name: REGR1WEIGHT=
arguments.description: specifies the weight for regularizing the R1 loss

arguments.name: TRUNCATION=
arguments.description: specifies the truncation ratio for image generation

arguments.name: USESMALLNETWORK
arguments.description: USESMALLNETWORK} specifies that small networks are to be used
